chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P No.D-2398 of 2019 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For order on office objection. 2. For hearing of main case. 06-12-2022 Mr. Javed Ali Buriro advocate for petitioners. Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, Additional Advocate General Sindh. Petitioners are paramedical staff working in Police Department as OT Technicians BPS-09 have claimed Health Professional Allowance @ 20% of the running basic pay in terms of a notification dated 03.04.2018. In response, comments have been filed by all concerned including Finance Department showing that on the strength of such notification Finance Department has also issued relevant order to the departments and now it is upto Home Department and Police Department in this case to comply with it. Further, the Finance Department has released the amount to the respective departments. A.I.G.P Legal in his comments dated 20.04.2022 has claimed that Additional Inspector General Police Welfare has requested to Medical Superintendents Police Hospital Hyderabad and Karachi to submit the bills (of such employees) in the office of Accountant General Sindh. These all facts show that there is no legal hitch or otherwise in granting the said allowance to the petitioners and in fact, in compliance of the notification, the requisite bills have been directed to be submitted before Accountant General. We, therefore, dispose of this petition with consent of learned A.A.G. Sindh directing AIGP Welfare to expedite the process of submitting the bills and granting the said allowance to the petitioners in the light of aforesaid notification and the comments dated 20.04.2022 filed by him.

We, therefore, dispose of this petition with consent of learned A.A.G. Sindh directing AIGP Welfare to expedite the process of submitting the bills and granting the said allowance to the petitioners in the light of aforesaid notification and the comments dated 20.04.2022 filed by him. JUDGE JUDGE *Abdullah Channa/PS*
